generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "360-degree LiDAR sensor with ±10mm measurement accuracy providing high-resolution spatial mapping and obstacle detection for autonomous vehicle environmental perception. Operates at 30Hz with Ethernet connection for 1Gbps data transmission."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing 640x480 resolution at 30FPS with sub-millimeter depth precision for 3D environmental perception. Synchronized with LiDAR at 30Hz via Ethernet connection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor suite capturing motor actuation, steering servo status, and ego vehicle dynamics for ACC system control. Provides real-time state information at ≥10Hz frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateInformation
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted via UDP/IP at ≥10Hz for object clustering and tracking. Processed through PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data transmitted via UDP/IP at 30Hz for 3D environmental perception. Processed through SensorFusionEnabler."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealsenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleStateInformation : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data (motor actuation, steering status, dynamics) transmitted via ROS2 at ≥10Hz for ACC control. Processed through DataTranslationFE."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from LiDAR clustering, transmitted via ROS2 topics for environmental perception. Generated through PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational geometry model for segmenting raw LiDAR point clouds into meaningful objects (vehicles, obstacles) using clustering algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Temporal tracking model maintaining object identities across consecutive LiDAR scans for 2D/3D space."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm executed on Digital Twin Entity for speed regulation and obstacle avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ProcessedEnvironmentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured environmental data combining processed LiDAR/object tracks and camera depth information for ACC decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData, DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state information including speed, acceleration, and dynamic parameters for system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateInformation
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing computational geometry algorithms for LiDAR point cloud clustering and object tracking at 30Hz. Uses ROS2 for inter-node communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity serializing data streams, performing unit conversions (LiDAR coordinates to metric units), and validating message integrity for interoperability."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SensorFusionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based node combining LiDAR and camera data for unified environmental perception with sub-millimeter precision."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 implementation providing 100ms latency message-passing between software components within Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler, DataTranslationFE, SensorFusionEnabler
]

instance UDPTransportLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi based UDP transport layer for real-time transmission of raw sensor data between Physical Twin and Digital Twin at 1Gbps bandwidth."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationFE
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack50 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded Linux platform with GPU acceleration on NVIDIA Jetson hardware for real-time computation of ACC algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured database for storing sensor logs, system metrics, and configuration parameters with ISO 23247 compliance."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry service providing continuous streaming of processed sensor data and system metrics for HIL operators."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentVisualizationInsight, SystemStatusInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing ACC control algorithms on Digital Twin Entity to reduce embedded system load while maintaining 100ms response time."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote reconfiguration of ACC parameters and centralized access to historical data for system administrators."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterConfigurationAction, DataRetrievalInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance EnvironmentVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of processed environmental data (objects, depth information) for human-in-the-loop monitoring."
]

instance SystemStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dashboard displaying real-time vehicle state metrics and system performance indicators for diagnostic purposes."
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic execution of speed regulation and obstacle avoidance algorithms based on processed environmental data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote adjustment of ACC parameters (cruise speed thresholds, obstacle detection settings) by system administrators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataRetrievalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Access to historical sensor data and system logs for post-mortem analysis and performance evaluation."
]

// C1: Entities
instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:PhysicalTwin [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle platform with onboard sensors and embedded computing for real-time cyber-physical experimentation."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealsenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Entity executing computational models and services for ACC system monitoring, control, and remote management."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler, DataTranslationFE, SensorFusionEnabler, ROS2CommunicationFramework, NVIDIAJetpack50, SQLDatabase
]

instance UserInterfaceEntity : DTDFVocab:UserInterface [
    base:desc "Entity providing 3D visualization and control panel interfaces for human-in-the-loop interaction with the Digital Twin."
    base:contains EnvironmentVisualizationInsight, SystemStatusInsight
]

// C12: Entity Relationships
instance PhysicalToVirtualInteraction : DTDFVocab:Interaction [
    base:desc "Real-time data transmission pipeline from Physical Twin sensors to Digital Twin Entity via UDP/IP and ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:between AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin, DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:uses UDPTransportLayer, ROS2CommunicationFramework
]

instance VirtualToPhysicalInteraction : DTDFVocab:Interaction [
    base:desc "Control commands from Digital Twin Entity to Physical Twin for ACC system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:between DigitalTwinEntity, AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin
]

// C13: Environment
instance AutonomousDrivingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational domain for 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle testing with real-time latency constraints (100ms)."
    base:contains AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin
]

instance DigitalTwinExecutionEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Computational environment hosting Digital Twin services and enablers on NVIDIA Jetson hardware."
    base:contains DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance UserInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment hosting user interface components for human-in-the-loop interaction."
    base:contains UserInterfaceEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is facilitated by the **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Controlling FE**, which computes control commands based on digital twin data (e.g., lead vehicle detection, projected driving path) and translates them into physical actuation. **Command types** include:
  - **Motor Actuation Commands**: Speed adjustments (e.g., throttle/brake signals) derived from speed control algorithms.
  - **Steering Servo Commands**: Directional adjustments based on path planning outputs.
  - **Vehicle State Feedback Loops**: Real-time adjustments to maintain stability (e.g., adaptive cruise control).

**Control message formats** are serialized via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures compatibility with physical actuators (e.g., converting ROS2 messages to actuator-specific protocols). **Communication protocols** mirror the physical-to-virtual flow:
  - **Intra-entity**: ROS2 for command routing within the Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Inter-entity**: Wi-Fi/UDP for transmitting commands to physical actuators (e.g., motor/steering servos).

**Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed in the documents, though the *Data Translation FE* likely enforces message integrity checks (e.g., checksums, timestamp validation). **Feedback systems** rely on bidirectional data flows: the Digital Twin Entity monitors **vehicle state** (e.g., speed, steering angle) via sensors and adjusts commands dynamically. **Conflict resolution** is implied to prioritize **safety-critical commands** (e.g., emergency braking) over non-critical adjustments, though explicit prioritization rules are absent. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through real-time synchronization of digital twin state (e.g., vehicle position) with physical sensors, ensuring consistency.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination. This temporal constraint is mandated due to hardware limitations and aligns with safety-critical vehicular application requirements, though stricter standards (e.g., <100 ms) are referenced in related ISO 23247-based architectures. The system’s temporal granularity is dictated by ROS2-based execution, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. Latency requirements for inter-node communication are implicitly governed by ROS2’s real-time capabilities, though explicit latency benchmarks for services (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) are not documented. The architecture does not explicitly detail service-specific time scales beyond the 100 ms synchronization baseline, which applies uniformly across the Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity components."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an independent ROS2 package containing one or more ROS2 nodes. This design adheres to ISO 23247’s entity-based structure but introduces deviations: the *Physical Twin Entity* aggregates hardware resources (e.g., sensors, actuators) and general automotive functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), contrasting with ISO 23247’s OMEs. The *Digital Twin Entity* manages data processing and control mechanisms, while the *User Entity* hosts user-facing components (*3D Visualization* and *Control Panel*). Orchestration is achieved through ROS2’s topic-based and service-based communication, with resource management implicitly handled by the entity’s hardware deployment. System-wide coordination relies on the 100 ms clock synchronization and ROS2’s deterministic execution model, though no explicit orchestration framework (e.g., Kubernetes, EdgeX) is mentioned. The architecture’s scalability is constrained by hardware-specific FE deployments, impacting engineering processes and functional ownership."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard defines Digital Twins as **real-time, high-fidelity computational reflections** of physical manufacturing elements, but it does not provide explicit methodologies for **model validation, verification, uncertainty quantification, or error correction**. The framework implicitly requires **consistency between OMEs and Digital Twin Entities** via observable attributes, though no formalized processes (e.g., comparative analysis, benchmark testing) or standards (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62424) are referenced for ensuring fidelity or quality assurance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined by a **multi-layered network architecture**:
  - **Intra-entity communication**: Uses **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** for software component interaction within the **Digital Twin Entity** and **User Entity**.
  - **Inter-entity communication**: Relies on **Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer** for data exchange between **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.
  - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a central hub for **serialization**, **message type alignment**, and **unit conversions** across all data streams (e.g., LiDAR → object tracks, vehicle state → control signals).
**Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not specified, but real-time **3D visualization** (Fig. 3a) and **ACC control** imply **low-latency (<100ms)** and **high-bandwidth** (≥10Mbps) for sensor data streams. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not mentioned, but the use of **Wi-Fi/UDP** suggests potential for packet loss mitigation via retransmission (not detailed). **Security protocols** are not explicitly described, though UDP’s stateless nature may require application-layer encryption (not referenced). The system’s **network topology** appears **distributed**, with entities communicating over a shared Wi-Fi network, while ROS2 ensures localized component coordination.

"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. Key assumptions from the context:
  - **Hosting platforms**: Likely **edge computing** (due to real-time sensor processing and visualization requirements) or **on-premise** (for localized vehicle systems), but **cloud deployment** is not mentioned.
  - **Computational requirements**: High-performance processing for **LiDAR clustering**, **point cloud tracking**, and **3D visualization** (Fig. 3a), suggesting **GPU-accelerated** systems (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or similar).
  - **Storage requirements**: Implicitly includes **data storage for reports** (via **Reporting FE**) and **point cloud/vehicle state logs**, but no specifics (e.g., capacity, persistence).
  - **Scalability mechanisms**: Not described, but the use of **ROS2** and **modular FE components** (e.g., Data Translation, Controlling FE) suggests **horizontal scalability** via distributed ROS2 nodes.
  - **Deployment models**: Likely **hybrid** (edge for real-time processing, cloud for analytics/reporting), but not confirmed. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not detailed, though real-time operation implies fault-tolerant design (e.g., redundant sensors or processing units, not mentioned).
**Not in Document** for precise details on cloud/edge split, redundancy strategies, or exact hardware specifications.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The document explicitly references the **ISO 23247:2021** standard, which defines:
    - **A Digital Twin framework for manufacturing**, structured into four parts (with **Part 2: Reference Architecture** being key).
    - **Entity-based RA** with roles like **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection/Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.
    - **Interoperability standards**: The standard allows flexibility in implementation (e.g., using **OPC UA** for data exchange).
    - **No explicit certification requirements** or compliance frameworks are mentioned, though adherence to ISO 23247 implies alignment with **industry standards for manufacturing DTs**.
    The standard does not detail **versioning** or **evolution governance** for DTs beyond modular design principles.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents adheres to **safety-critical requirements** as outlined in **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, emphasizing **performance and functional safety** in intelligent transport systems. While not explicitly referencing **ISO 26262**, the system implicitly aligns with **safety-critical automotive standards** due to its role in **real-time vehicle control**, where **clock synchronization** is critical for ensuring temporal consistency in safety-critical operations.

The documents highlight **safety-critical system considerations** in the context of **interactive Digital Twin cockpits** (as per [37]), where **real-time data synchronization** and **deterministic behavior** are essential for maintaining operational integrity. However, no explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., RBAC, MFA, HSMs), **authentication systems**, **threat mitigation strategies**, or **ASIL decomposition** is provided. The focus remains on **performance validation** (e.g., ISO 15622) and **functional safety** in **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** (per [36]), where **safety-critical communication** is implied but not detailed in terms of cryptographic or access control mechanisms.

No evidence of **hardware/software security measures**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **risk assessment frameworks** (e.g., ISO 26262) is present in the provided documents. The system’s safety considerations are **implicitly tied to automotive standards** (e.g., ISO 15622) rather than explicitly documented in the provided texts."
]
